title: Abcotronics Purchasing Policy
body:
COMPANY PURCHASING POLICY

Purpose

To ensure proper documentation, timely reporting, and effective control of all company purchases—including credit card purchases, EFT transactions, petrol cards, store accounts, and other purchasing methods. This policy supports financial integrity and budget management.

Scope

This policy applies to all employees engaged in company purchasing, regardless of the method used (credit card, EFT, petrol card, store accounts, etc.).

Definition of Company Expenses

Company Expenses: Expenditures made for the benefit of the company, including office supplies, travel expenses, client entertainment, and any other costs directly related to business operations.

Personal Expenses: Any expenditure not related to company operations, such as personal meals, non-business-related travel, and personal goods.

Submission of Purchase Documentation

Credit Card Purchases:

Within 3 Days: Credit card slips must be submitted to the Finance Department via DEXT within 3 days of the purchase.

Monthly Deadline: All credit card slips for purchases made within the month must be submitted by the last day of the month.

EFT Transactions, Petrol Cards, and Store Accounts:

Documentation (receipts, invoices) must be submitted to the Finance Department within 3 days of the transaction, including all supporting details and proper allocation.

Documentation Requirements

Receipts/Invoices: Must show date, amount, and description of the purchase.

Clear Uploads: All slips and invoices need to be clear and complete when uploaded to DEXT. Blurry or incomplete submissions will not be accepted.

Allocation: Each purchase must be allocated to a specific Class (Department) and/or Customer within DEXT or other designated platforms. Ensure to select the appropriate customer and include a description where necessary.

Cost Of Sales Breakdown: Employees must use designated codes for different types of purchases (fuel, travel, parts, etc.) to ensure proper categorization.

Full Tax Invoices: For any purchases over R5,000, a full tax invoice must be provided. Invoices must meet the following criteria:

Contains the words “Tax Invoice,” “VAT Invoice,” or “Invoice”

Name, address, and VAT registration number of the supplier

Name, address, and VAT registration number of the recipient, if applicable

Serial number and date of issue of the invoice

Accurate description of goods/services

Quantity or volume of goods/services supplied

Value of the supply, amount of tax charged, and consideration of the supply

Abridged Tax Invoices: For purchases between R50 and R5,000, invoices must include:

Contains the words “Tax Invoice,” “VAT Invoice,” or “Invoice”

Name, address, and VAT registration number of the supplier

Serial number and date of issue of the invoice

Accurate description of goods/services

Value of the supply, amount of tax charged, and consideration of the supply

Meal and Daily Rate Limits

Employees must adhere to meal and daily expenditure limits established by management, which will include allowances for breakfast, lunch, and dinner. Each employee’s specific limits will be determined based on business needs and communicated accordingly.

Employees may combine breakfast and lunch expenses if total daily spending does not exceed the allowable amount. Adjustments to the values may be discussed with management as needed.

Tips for gratuity may not exceed 10% of the total meal cost.

Safekeeping and Control of Credit Cards

Credit cards and credit card numbers must be kept secure at all times. Employees must:

Store credit cards in a secure location when not in use.

Report any lost or stolen cards immediately to the Finance Department.

Avoid sharing credit card information or using it for unauthorized transactions.

Purchase Procedures and Limits

Purchases Over R2,000: Contact the Finance Department to ensure availability of funds and provide reasons for the expenditure.

Purchases Over R5,000: Obtain written authorization from the Head of Department (HOD) before engaging with Finance.

Purchases Over R10,000: Obtain written authorization from the CEO before proceeding.

Consequences for Non-Compliance

Salary Deduction: For failure to submit credit card slips within 60 days after a transaction, the purchase amount will be deducted from the cardholder’s salary.

End-of-Month Deadline: Failure to meet the monthly submission deadline may result in deductions from the cardholder’s salary for unsubstantiated purchases. Deductions will occur until the last salary payment date of the month. If slips are uploaded after this date, they may be deducted from the following month's salary unless prior arrangements have been made with Finance.

Responsibility

Cardholders and all employees involved in purchasing must ensure proper documentation and reporting according to this policy. Employees must keep copies of all receipts and invoices for a period agreed upon with management. For purchases over R5,000, a signature from the HOD is required.

Exceptions

Any exceptions to this policy must be approved in advance by the Finance Department, with a valid reason provided in writing.

Policy Review

This policy will be reviewed annually and updated as necessary to reflect changes in company procedures or regulations.

Contact

For questions or clarifications, contact the Finance Department.

Acknowledgment Form

By signing below, I acknowledge that I have read, understood, and agree to comply with the Company Purchasing Policy.

Employee Name:

Employee Signature:

Date:

title: Abcotronics Client Communication Policy
body:
Abcotronics Client Communication Guidelines

When communicating with Clients, Abcotronics staff and contractors must adhere to the following guidelines:

Professionalism

Respect and Courtesy: Always address clients respectfully, using appropriate titles and their preferred names (including correct spelling). Start and end communications with polite greetings and sign-offs.

Language and Tone: Use clear, concise, and professional language. Avoid slang, jargon, or overly technical terms unless you are certain the client understands them. Aim to be polite and respectful in every interaction.

Spelling and Grammar: Ensure your spelling and grammar is correct.

Positive Attitude:

Maintain a positive and helpful attitude, even in challenging situations. Demonstrate a willingness to assist and resolve any issues the client may have.

Responsiveness

Timely Responses: Unless we are bound to provide responses sooner (Support Team have prescribed turn around times), aim to respond to client inquiries within 24 hours. For urgent matters, strive to respond as soon as possible. If a quick response is not feasible, send a brief acknowledgment to let the client know their inquiry has been received.

Example: "Thank you for your email. I will look into this and get back to you by the end of the day."

Follow-Up: If we are waiting on something from a client, follow up regularly by striking a balance between being persistent but polite.

Clarity and Transparency

Agenda / Planning: Before engaging with any client, where feasible, create a written agenda which will guide discussions. This agenda may be shared with the client prior to a meeting, if necessary.

Clear Information: Provide clear and accurate information. Break down complex information into understandable parts, using bullet points or numbered lists if necessary.

Example: "Here are the steps we will take to resolve your issue: 1. Review your profile. 2. Identify discrepancies. 3. Correct errors."

Considered, Coherent Communication: Ensure your discussions are to the point, remain on topic.

Honesty: Be honest about what you can and cannot do. Avoid making promises that cannot be kept. If you do not have an answer, let the client know you will find out and get back to them.

Example: "I’m not sure about that, but I will find out and get back to you by tomorrow."

Transparency: Clearly communicate processes, timelines, and any costs involved. Ensure the client understands each step of the process and what to expect.

Confidentiality

Privacy: Ensure client information is kept confidential and shared only with authorized personnel. Avoid discussing client details in public or unsecured environments.

Data Security: Follow company protocols for data protection and security when handling client information.

Empathy and Understanding

Listen Actively: Listen to client concerns without interrupting. Show that you are fully engaged and understand their needs. Do not interrupt clients, rather take notes and raise your concerns after they have completed their explanation.

Empathy: Show empathy and understanding, especially when clients are facing difficulties or frustrations. Acknowledge their feelings and reassure them that you are there to help.

Consistency

Uniform Communication: Ensure all team members communicate consistently, following the same guidelines and tone. This ensures clients receive a cohesive experience.

Documentation: Keep records of client communications to ensure consistency and continuity. This helps in providing accurate and timely responses.

Problem Resolution

Proactive Approach: Address potential issues before they escalate. Anticipate client needs and take steps to prevent problems.

Solutions-Oriented: Focus on finding solutions and resolving issues promptly. Offer practical solutions and involve the client in the decision-making process where appropriate.

Assist, Don’t Accuse: Our primary goal is to solve clients’ problems, not to point out their mistakes. Approach every issue with the intent to help and provide constructive solutions.

Example: Instead of saying, "You didn’t provide the correct information," say, "Let’s gather the correct information together to proceed."

Feedback and Improvement

Encourage Feedback: Regularly ask for client feedback to improve services and communication.

Continuous Improvement: Use feedback to make continuous improvements in communication and service delivery. Share client feedback with the team to foster a culture of improvement.

title: Abcotronics Company Computer and Mobile Device Policy
body:
ABCOTRONICS COMPANY COMPUTER AND MOBILE DEVICE POLICY

Computer and Mobile Device Use Policy

Scope

This policy applies to all Abcotronics employees, contractors, interns, and any other individuals (hereinafter “Employees”) who use our organization’s technology. Without limitation, this policy applies to:

All computers owned or leased by Abcotronics

All cell phones owned or leased by Abcotronics

All land line telephones owned or leased by Abcotronics

All other technological devices owned or leased by Abcotronics

All networks owned or managed by Abcotronics.

All telephone numbers owned or leased by Abcotronics

All messaging and email platforms owned or operated by Abcotronics

Any other communication / IT system owned or operated by Abcotronics.

This policy does not include your personal device. However, information / data transmitted from personal devices on the Abcotronics network shall be subject to this policy.

Purpose

The purpose of this policy is to protect both the organization’s assets and the privacy of its employees. It aims to promote productivity, maintain employee privacy, and prevent unauthorized access to company devices and networks.

Acceptable Uses and Restrictions

Employees are allowed to use company devices for work purposes only.

Employees are allowed to use company internet and networks for work- related purposes only. Internet access is permitted for minor personal use for necessary communications only.

Employees are not permitted to use company devices, networks or internet to stream any video or audio content unless incidental to work

Emails and/messages sent from company devices / email addresses should only relate to Abcotronics business endeavors and be aimed at advancing the interests of Abcotronics. Personal emails / messages must be sent from personal email addresses / personal devices.

Employees can only install software that is approved by the IT department or management.

Employees are not permitted to delete any business related information stored on a company issued device / email server / cloud storage without permission of IT or management.

Abcotronics Rights

Abcotronics reserves the right to:

Immediately take possession of any company devices

Immediately conduct a search any company devices

Remotely connect to any company devices

Immediately and without notice access any email address owned by abcotronics.co.za

Immediately and without notice, access any WhatsApp or other messaging services owned by Abcotronics

Monitor internet traffic on company owned devices

Monitor calls etc on company owned devices

Cease provision of a company device to any Employee.

Consequences

Violations of this policy may result in disciplinary action, up to and including termination of employment.

Unauthorized disclosure of confidential information will result in legal action.

Procedures for reporting violations

Employees should report any violations of this policy to management.

This policy is subject to change from time to time, provided notice is given to Employees.

Date:

Employee Acceptance:

title: Employee NDA, Non-Compete and Non-Solicitation
body:
CONFIDENTIALITY NON-COMPETITION AND NON-SOLICITATION AGREEMENT

entered into by and between

ABCOTRONICS (PTY) LTD

(the “Company”)

and

FULL NAME:

IDENTITY NUMBER:

(the “Employee”) together the “Parties”.

DEFINITIONS AND INTERPRETATION

In this Agreement, unless a contrary intention clearly appears, the following terms shall have the meaning assigned to them hereunder, namely:

“Competitor” means any organisation, individual, partnership or other business that carries on business substantially similar to or the same as the Company and is not in any way affiliated with the Company;

"Confidential Information" means any information or data which by its nature or content is identifiable as confidential and/or proprietary to the Company or which is provided or disclosed in confidence, and which the Company or any person acting on behalf of the Company may disclose or provide to the Employee or which may come to the knowledge of the Employee by whatsoever means. Without limitation, confidential information shall include the following even if it is not marked as being ‘confidential’, restricted or proprietary (or any similar designation) -

information of the Company’s client base and any information pertaining to any such clients and or any matter such clients are involved in or requires assistance in;

information relating to the Company's strategic objectives and plans for both its existing and planned future business;

information relating to the Company's business activities, business relationships, origination models, products, services, processes, data, and staff;

strategy documents, financial information (including valuations and forecasts) and business knowledge of the Company,

agreements to which the Company is a party;

the Company's technical and scientific information;

the Company's commercial, financial and market information and trade secrets;

Intellectual Property that is proprietary to the Company or that is proprietary to a third party and in respect of which the Company has rights of use or possession, including but not limited to data;

information contained in or constituting the Company's hardware or software, including third party products, and associated Material;

information relating to the Company's network, telecommunications services and facilities;

information concerning faults or defects in the Company's systems, hardware, software, network, telecommunication services and/or facilities, or the incidence of such faults or defects; and

any and all, methodologies, formulae, related information and trade secrets in processes and other business of the Company.

The Confidential Information excludes information or data which -

is lawfully in the public domain at the time of disclosure thereof; or

subsequently independently becomes lawfully part of the public domain by publication or otherwise; or

becomes available from a source other than the Company which is lawfully entitled without any restriction on disclosure to disclose such Confidential Information; or

is disclosed pursuant to a requirement by operation of law, regulation or court order;

provided that -

the onus shall at all times rest on the Party to whom information has been disclosed to establish that such information falls within such exclusions; and

the information disclosed will not be deemed to be within the foregoing exclusions merely because such information is embraced by more general information in the public domain or in a Party's possession; and

any combination of features will not be deemed to be within the foregoing exclusions merely because individual features are in the public domain or in a Party's possession, but only if the combination itself is in the public domain or in a Party's possession.

The determination of whether information is Confidential Information shall not be affected by whether or not such information is subject to, or protected by, common law or statute related to copyright, patent, trade marks or otherwise.

"Copyright Material" means any Material in which copyright subsists;

“Dealings” means any interaction the Employee may have with the Company’s customers, staff, Confidential Information or suppliers during the course of employment at the Company;

"Effective Date" means the date of last signature;

"Intellectual Property" means any know-how (not in the public domain), invention (whether or not patented), design, trade mark, or Copyright Material (whether or not registered), goodwill, processes, process methodology and all other identical or similar intellectual property as may exist anywhere in the world and any applications for registration of such intellectual property;

"Material" means any direct or indirect expression of thought or of an idea or of a combination of thoughts or ideas in material form, any direct or indirect artistic or aesthetic expression in material form or any data in material form, all reports, documentation, information, software or inventions, irrespective of the media on which they occur, and includes, without limiting the generality thereof, all process maps in whatsoever medium, all written and printed material, all micro-graphic and other reproductions of the written word, depiction and pictorial material, and all audio-visual, machine-readable and other information;

"Parties" means the Company and the Employee;

"Personnel" means officers, employees, agents, partners, directors, advisors or independent contractors of the Parties that are employed, mandated or contracted; and

“Territory” means Worldwide.

The clause headings in this Agreement have been inserted for convenience only and will not be taken into consideration in its interpretation.

In this Agreement, unless a contrary intention clearly appears:

words importing any one gender include the other two genders;

words importing the singular include the plural and vice versa; and

reference to natural persons include juristic persons (which includes trusts) and vice versa;

If any provision in this clause 2 is a substantive provision conferring rights or imposing obligations on any Party, then notwithstanding that such provision is contained in clause 2, effect shall be given thereto as if such provision were a substantive provision in the body of the agreement.

The terms of this Agreement have been negotiated and the rule of interpretation which construes the interpretation of the agreement against the drafter thereof shall not be applicable thereto.

Any reference to an enactment is to that enactment as at the date of signature hereof and as amended or re-enacted from time to time.

When any number of days is prescribed in this Agreement, same shall be reckoned exclusively of the first and inclusively of the last day unless the last day falls on a Saturday, Sunday or public holiday, in which case the last day shall be the next business day;

Where figures are referred to in numerals and in words, and there is any conflict between the two, the words shall prevail, unless the context indicates a contrary intention.

DISCLOSURE AND USE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION

The Employee acknowledges -

that the Company has a commercial or other interest in all such Confidential Information as it may disclose to the Employee, or that may be disclosed to the Employee during any Dealings;

that the Confidential Information is of great importance to the Company;

that the Confidential Information is a valuable, special and unique asset; and

that the Company may suffer irreparable harm or substantial economic and other loss in the event of such Confidential Information being disclosed or used otherwise than in accordance with this Agreement.

The Employee agrees and undertakes -

not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than in connection with their employment with the Company, and then on a “need to know” basis only;

not to use the Confidential Information, whether directly or indirectly, for its own benefit or for the benefit of any person other than the Company;

to treat and safeguard Confidential Information as strictly private and confidential;

except as permitted by this Agreement, not to use, disclose or divulge, directly or indirectly, the Confidential Information in any manner to any third party for any reason or purpose whatsoever without the prior written consent of the Company, which consent may be withheld in the sole and absolute discretion of the Company;

not to decompile, disassemble or reverse engineer the whole or any part of the Confidential Information;

to take all such steps as may be reasonably necessary to prevent Confidential Information from falling into the hands of unauthorised third parties;

The obligations of the Employee with respect to each item of Confidential Information shall endure for an indefinite period from receipt of that item of Confidential Information.

TITLE AND PROPRIETARY RIGHTS

All Confidential Information disclosed or which becomes known to the Employee, is acknowledged:

to be proprietary to the Company;

not to confer any rights of whatsoever nature in such Confidential Information on the Employee.

Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights by licence or otherwise in any information disclosed except as provided herein.

TERM AND TERMINATION

This Agreement shall come into force on the Effective Date and the obligations of:

confidentiality set out in this Agreement shall continue indefinitely; and

non-competition and non-solicitation set out in this Agreement shall continue for the relevant periods set out in clause 6 and 7 respectively.

As fully detailed in clause 5, following the expiry or termination of this Agreement, the Employee shall return all Confidential Information to the Company and shall ensure that no copies are retained.

RETURN OF INFORMATION

At any time the Company may require the Employee to immediately return all copies and partial copies of all Confidential Information (whether in paper, electronic or other format) which the Employee may have obtained from the Company as well as all notes (whether in paper, electronic or other format) which the Employee may have prepared or may have obtained as a result of the Confidential Information being made available, and to furnish a written statement to the effect that, upon such return, the Employee has not retained any such Confidential Information in its possession, or under its control, either directly or indirectly.

The Employee shall comply with a request, in terms of clause 5.2 within 7 (seven) days of receipt of such a request.

NON-SOLICITATION OF CUSTOMERS, SUPPLIERS AND EMPLOYEES

Subject to clause 6.2 below, the Employee shall not, during or for a period of 2 years following the termination of employment (for any reason whatsoever), solicit any of the Company’s customers, suppliers or employees.

The restriction in clause 6.1 can be waived on a per-customer, per-supplier or per-employee basis by the Company in writing on written request from the Employee, provided that such consent shall be at the sole discretion of the Company. No waiver may be given if it shall violate any prior agreement between the relevant customer, supplier or employee and the Company as to the sharing of the customer’s, supplier’s or employee’s details.

No waiver given under clause 6.2 shall affect the provisions of clause 7 of this Agreement.

NON-COMPETITION

The Employee shall not, for a period of 3 years following the completion of all business dealings with the Company, provide like services to any Competitor within the Territory.

BREACH

Both Parties hereby acknowledge that damages alone are unlikely to be an adequate remedy for any breach by the Employee of this Agreement.

The Company shall, without prejudice to any and all other rights and remedies which may be available, be entitled to the remedies of interdict, specific performance and other relief for any breach of this Agreement by the Employee, actual or threatened.

In addition to any rights or remedies whether at law or in equity to which the Company may be entitled, the Employee hereby agrees to indemnify the Company against any and all liabilities and financial or other loss, damage, costs or expenses (including legal costs on a full indemnity basis), direct or indirect, including loss of profit, which may arise out of its breach of this Agreement.

COUNTERPARTS

This Agreement may be entered into in any number of counterparts and by the Parties to it on separate counterparts each of which when so executed and delivered will be an original, but all the counterparts together will constitute one and the same instrument.

SEVERANCE

In the event that one or more of the provisions of this Agreement is found to be unlawful, invalid or otherwise unenforceable, that / those provision(s) will be deemed severed from the remainder of this Agreement. The remainder of this Agreement will be valid and enforceable.

ANNOUNCEMENTS

No Party shall have the right to advertise or make public or cause to be publicised any statement concerning the existence or terms of this Agreement or the relationship between the Parties unless such statement, publication or advertisement has been approved in advance by the other Parties.

ADDRESS FOR LEGAL NOTICES

The Parties choose as their addresses and contact details for all purposes under this Agreement, whether in respect of court process, notices or other documents or communications of whatsoever nature (including but not limited to the exercise of any option and/or right of first refusal set out in this Agreement), the following addresses:

The Company:

Address:120 Roberts Road, Pietermarizburg, 3201

Tel:081 517 7387

Email:  david@abcotronics.co.za

The Employee:

Address:

Tel:

Email:

Any notice or communications required or permitted to be given in terms of this Agreement shall be valid and effective only if in writing but it shall be competent to give notice by email.

Any Party may by notice to all the other Parties change the physical address chosen in this clause 20 to another physical address or its email address provided that the change shall become effective on the fifth Business Day from the deemed receipt of the notice by the last of the Parties.

Any document:

sent by prepaid registered post in a correctly addressed envelope to any Party at chosen address shall be deemed to have been received on the seventh day after posting (unless the contrary is proven);

delivered by hand to a responsible person during ordinary business hours at its chosen address shall be deemed to have been received on the day of delivery; or

sent by email to its chosen email address shall be deemed to have been received on the date of dispatch and at the time recorded by the computer used by the sender (unless the contrary is proven).

Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein contained, a written notice or communication actually received by a Party shall be an adequate written notice or communication to it notwithstanding that it was not sent to or delivered at its chosen address.

TERMINATION

This Agreement will terminate only by mutual agreement between the Parties.

APPLICABLE LAW AND JURISDICTION

This Agreement will in all respects be governed by and construed under the laws of the Republic of South Africa.

COSTS

Each Party shall be liable for its own legal costs associated with the preparation and conclusion of this Agreement.

SEVERABILITY

Any provision in this Agreement which is or may become illegal, invalid or unenforceable in any jurisdiction affected by this Agreement shall, as to such jurisdiction, be ineffective to the extent of such prohibition or unenforceability and shall be treated as if it had not been written and severed from the balance of this Agreement, without invalidating the remaining provisions of this Agreement or affecting the validity or enforceability of such provision in any other jurisdiction.

SIGNED ATTHISDAY OF2024.

AS WITNESSES:

EMPLOYEE

AS WITNESSES:

DAVID BUTTEMER for and on behalf of Abcotronics (Pty) Ltd, who warrants that he is duly authorized to sign on behalf of Abcotronics (Pty) Ltd
